exp-table1.summary: Case study comparing top-p vs min-p token probability behavior. Case Study 1 (low-confidence prompt about a battle scene): at τ=3, both top-p and min-p preserve the top tokens 'was' (top-p=13.1%, min-p=18.5%), 'lasted' (6.7% / 9.5%), 'between' (5.9% / 8.2%), 'left' (5.3% / 7.4%), 'would' (4.7% / 6.6%), 'seemed' (2.5% / 3.5%); min-p maintains diversity similar to top-p. Case Study 2 (high-confidence prompt about rainbow refraction): at τ=3, top-p retains 'light' (38.2%), 'sunlight' (9.0%), 'water' (3.8%), 'sunshine' (3.2%), 'a' (3.0%), 'moisture' (3.0%); min-p filters aggressively to only 'light' (80.9%) and 'sunlight' (19.1%), removing semantically less-relevant tokens. This demonstrates min-p's dynamic threshold: it relaxes in low-confidence contexts and constrains in high-confidence ones.

exp-figure2.summary: Accuracy vs Average Entropy of Correct Predictions (creativity) on GSM8K CoT-SC, Mistral-7B. Min-p configurations (circles, 20 hyperparam points) consistently lie in the upper-right region (higher accuracy + higher entropy), forming a Pareto frontier above and to the right of top-p's (triangles, 20 points). Top-p configurations cluster tightly with low spread. Min-p frontier is closer to the ideal top-right corner, dominating top-p across the accuracy-diversity trade-off.

exp-table4.summary: Human evaluation Quality and Diversity scores (1-10 scale, mean ± std error). LOW DIVERSITY SETTINGS [Standard / Top-p=0.1 / Min-p=0.2]: T=1 Quality 7.55±0.24 / 5.96±0.31 / 7.06±0.20, Diversity 7.08±0.29 / 2.40±0.28 / 5.83±0.28; T=2 Quality 7.76±0.26 / 5.43±0.31 / 7.62±0.21, Diversity 7.66±0.25 / 1.83±0.22 / 6.91±0.27; T=3 Quality 6.75±0.35 / 5.75±0.32 / 7.74±0.24, Diversity 7.04±0.28 / 2.25±0.34 / 7.60±0.26. HIGH DIVERSITY SETTINGS [Standard / Top-p=0.9 / Min-p=0.05]: T=1 Quality 7.96±0.15 / 7.67±0.19 / 8.02±0.19, Diversity 7.66±0.20 / 7.04±0.26 / 7.74±0.22; T=2 Quality 7.85±0.19 / 7.75±0.19 / 7.98±0.20, Diversity 7.57±0.21 / 7.66±0.21 / 7.96±0.21; T=3 Quality 6.83±0.29 / 7.11±0.29 / 7.57±0.23 (significant p<0.05), Diversity 7.43±0.28 / 7.49±0.24 / 7.66±0.20. Min-p means exceed top-p across all 12 (T, diversity) pairs.

exp-table7.summary: Ablation on AlpacaEval Creative Writing across temperatures (Llama 3 70B). Min-p generally outperforms top-p across temperature settings, with highest winrate achieved by min-p=0.1 at τ=1.5. Winrate (LC) results consistent with raw Winrate, confirming benefits not biased by output length.

exp-table9.summary: Llama 3.1 70B results (Appendix). Min-p sampling matches or exceeds top-p across temperatures on GPQA Main and GSM8K CoT, confirming method generalizes across model families.

exp-table12.summary: Ablation combining min-p with other truncation methods (top-p + min-p). Result: double normalization issues lead to suboptimal performance because hyperparameters are tuned for standalone use. Paper recommends min-p as primary truncation method.

exp-table8.summary: Llama 3 models comparison: min-p sampling outperforms top-p on GPQA and GSM8K CoT benchmarks, with larger gains at higher temperatures, confirming cross-family generalization.
